# Preface {.unnumbered}

Welcome to **A Modern R-proach to Econometrics**.

This digital book serves as a comprehensive computational companion to Jeffrey Wooldridge's *Introductory Econometrics: A Modern Approach*. As a Teaching Assistant at **Alexandria University**, I developed this resource to bridge the gap between theoretical econometric concepts and practical data analysis using the **R programming language**.

## About the Author

I am **Ahmed H. Darwish**, a Teaching Assistant with a Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, majoring in **Applied Statistics**. Currently, I am pursuing my Master’s degree and conducting research in the field of econometrics.

My work focuses on:\
\* **Applied Econometrics:** Replicating complex academic models to ensure reproducibility.\
\* **Causal Inference:** Applying modern frameworks from "The Effect" to real-world data.\
\* **Open Source Contribution:** I am a contributor to the `wooldridge` Python library, and I bring that same rigor to this R-based project.

## Exercises Solved

| Chapter   | Total Questions | Solved |
|-----------|-----------------|--------|
| 1         | 8               | 3      |
| 2         | 11              | 3      |
| 3         | 13              | 3      |
| 4         | 14              | 3      |
| 5         | 6               | 3      |
| 6         | 14              | \-     |
| 7         | 17              | \-     |
| 8         | 14              | \-     |
| 9         | 14              | \-     |
| 10        | 14              | \-     |
| 11        | 14              | \-     |
| 12        | 16              | \-     |
| 13        | 16              | \-     |
| 14        | 17              | \-     |
| 15        | 13              | \-     |
| 16        | 12              | \-     |
| 17        | 17              | \-     |
| 18        | 16              | \-     |
| 19        | \-              | \-     |
| **Total** | **246**         | **15** |

: Exercises Solved

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*This project is a work in progress as part of my Master's thesis portfolio. For collaborations or inquiries regarding remote data analysis, feel free to contact me via GitHub.*
